privacy_boundary: Public repository state and commit metadata only. Raw private chat, health information, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.

records.subject: Canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE baseline versus later append-only continuity updates

observations.statement: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json still records M-001 as completed and names the next continuity action as adding commit and decision-history records plus linked contradiction modeling.
Later continuity commits added verified queue-candidate materialization and recorded its queue and evidence outputs without rewriting the canonical queue.

A reader consulting only ACTIVE_QUEUE.json could incorrectly infer that no continuity work occurred after M-001, while a reader consulting only recent commits could incorrectly infer that the canonical queue was formally compacted.

resolution.interpretation: The sources describe different state layers rather than mutually exclusive facts: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is the reviewed baseline, while later queue-update and evidence commits are append-only candidate state pending explicit compaction.
resolution.preservation_rule: Retain both sources unchanged, link them through this ledger, and prohibit treating append-only candidate state as canonical until a reviewed compaction commit explicitly updates ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.

records.claim_ceiling: Repository-state reconciliation only; does not prove private-history content, semantic correctness of every queue update, deployment success, tests outside recorded evidence, or peer execution.
records.review_route: During the next reviewed queue compaction, compare the baseline digest and all accepted append-only updates, then mark this record superseded only after ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains the reviewed materialized state.
